steps.label: What They Do
steps.body:
MediaMelon's SmartSight platform is video-first streaming intelligence: real-time Quality-of-Experience (QoE) analytics, ad monetization intelligence, and AI-powered session insights. Their unified metric is StreamScore™.

Integrates with 40+ player SDKs across CTV, mobile, web, and set-top boxes. Trusted by platforms supporting 20M+ concurrent viewers.

steps.label: How They Run on Akamai
steps.body:
SmartSight's analytics pipeline runs on LKE — elastically scaling to absorb live event traffic spikes without pre-provisioning. NodeBalancers distribute telemetry ingestion across SmartSight's microservices with no single point of failure.

Real example of LKE absorbing burst load (FIFA matches, NFL games) instead of standing capacity 24/7.

steps.label: When To Position
steps.body: Any OTT, CTV, FAST, or live operator who is flying blind on player-side QoE. Differentiate from generic RUM (mPulse) by emphasising video-specific metrics: rebuffering ratio, ABR ladder utilisation, ad fill rate.
"MediaMelon = video-first QoE analytics on LKE. Generic RUM is HTML — MediaMelon is video."

quiz.question: A streaming platform already uses Akamai mPulse. Why also bring in MediaMelon?

options.text: mPulse covers HTML/page metrics; MediaMelon covers video-specific QoE (rebuffering, ABR, ad fill)
options.explain: Different layers. mPulse = page experience. MediaMelon = video session experience.
mPulse works with any page, including video pages — it just doesn't track video-internal metrics.
Adding a tool isn't a cost-reduction story.

quiz.question: Why does LKE matter for MediaMelon's pipeline?

options.explain: Player SDKs run on the client device, not LKE.

options.text: Elastic scaling absorbs live-event telemetry spikes without pre-provisioning capacity
options.explain: Live events generate massive bursts. LKE + HPA scales pods just-in-time.
QoE math is software — runs anywhere. LKE handles operations and scale.
No — the SDKs still run client-side.
